speaker: doctor
text: Good morning. Let's start with your vitals — your heart rate today is 121 beats per minute, which is elevated for resting conditions.

speaker: patient
text: Oh, that’s higher than usual. I’ve been feeling a bit off all morning.

speaker: doctor
text: Your diastolic blood pressure is 64 mmHg — on the lower side, especially given your history.

speaker: doctor
text: Based on your symptoms, ECG findings, and prior angiogram, we’re confirming Coronary Artery Disease as your primary diagnosis today.

speaker: patient
text: That makes sense — I’ve had some chest tightness lately, especially when walking up stairs.

speaker: doctor
text: Have you noticed any other symptoms — shortness of breath, dizziness, or changes in energy level?

speaker: doctor
text: Your blood glucose came back at 13.8 mmol/L — significantly above target range.

speaker: patient
text: Yes — I’ve been extremely tired these past few days, even after a full night’s sleep.

speaker: patient
text: I’ve been having fatigue constantly since last week.

speaker: doctor
text: I’m prescribing Amlodipine 10mg once daily to help manage your blood pressure and coronary vasospasm.

speaker: doctor
text: Actually, your repeat glucose reading just now is 16.0 mmol/L — that’s even higher than the earlier value, so we need to address this urgently.

speaker: patient
text: I didn’t eat anything before coming in — just black coffee — so I’m surprised it’s that high.

speaker: doctor
text: Given the persistent hyperglycemia and your CAD diagnosis, I’m going to start you on Metformin 500mg twice daily, in addition to lifestyle counseling.

speaker: patient
text: Okay — should I stop taking my old diabetes medication? I was on gliclazide before.

speaker: doctor
text: Yes, we’ll discontinue gliclazide for now — Metformin is safer in your cardiovascular context.

speaker: doctor
text: By the way, your diastolic pressure has risen to 79 mmHg — quite a jump from the 64 we saw earlier. That may reflect improved peripheral resistance or hydration status, but we’ll monitor closely.

speaker: patient
text: I did drink two glasses of water while waiting — maybe that helped?

speaker: doctor
text: Possibly. We’ll recheck both BP and glucose at your follow-up in one week. In the meantime, please log meals, activity, and any symptoms.

speaker: patient
text: Will do. Also, I checked my glucose again at home this morning — same meter, same technique — and got 13.8 mmol/L.

speaker: patient
text: That’s the same number as the first reading you mentioned — that reading.
